title: Quiz: Single Qubit Gates
description: Test your understanding of single qubit quantum gates

questions.question: What property must all quantum gates satisfy?
questions.explanation: Quantum gates must be unitary matrices, meaning U†U = UU† = I. This ensures the operation is reversible and preserves the normalization of quantum states.

questions.question: What does the Pauli X gate do?
questions.explanation: The Pauli X gate is the quantum NOT gate. It flips the computational basis states: X|0⟩ = |1⟩ and X|1⟩ = |0⟩.

questions.question: What is the result of applying the Hadamard gate to |0⟩?
questions.explanation: The Hadamard gate creates an equal superposition: H|0⟩ = (|0⟩ + |1⟩)/√2, also called the |+⟩ state.

questions.question: The Hadamard gate is its own inverse: H² = I.
questions.explanation: True! Applying the Hadamard gate twice returns the state to its original form. This is because H is both unitary and Hermitian (H = H†).

questions.question: What does the Pauli Z gate do to the state |+⟩ = (|0⟩ + |1⟩)/√2?
questions.explanation: Z|+⟩ = Z(|0⟩ + |1⟩)/√2 = (Z|0⟩ + Z|1⟩)/√2 = (|0⟩ - |1⟩)/√2 = |-⟩. The Z gate flips the sign of the |1⟩ component.

questions.question: On the Bloch sphere, where is the |0⟩ state located?
questions.explanation: The |0⟩ state is at the north pole of the Bloch sphere (+z direction). The |1⟩ state is at the south pole (−z direction).

questions.question: Which gate adds a phase of i to the |1⟩ state while leaving |0⟩ unchanged?
questions.explanation: The S gate (also called √Z) applies a phase of i (90°) to the |1⟩ state: S|1⟩ = i|1⟩, while S|0⟩ = |0⟩.

questions.question: Which of the following are Pauli gates? (Select all that apply)
questions.explanation: The three Pauli gates are X, Y, and Z. The Hadamard gate (H) is not a Pauli gate, though it's closely related.
